title: Smoky Charred Japanese Pork Ramen
description: A charred, fluffy, and adventurous Japanese Kyushu dish featuring shredded meat, infused oil, and sugar-free friendly ingredients, this Smoky Charred Japanese Pork Ramen is a must try for any food enthusiast.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, smoked paprika, and sesame oil.
Add the pork shoulder to the bowl and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Remove the pork from the marinade and place it on a baking sheet lined with aluminum foil.
Roast the pork in the oven for 20 minutes per pound, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 190 degrees Fahrenheit.
While the pork is cooking, cook the ramen noodles according to the package instructions.
Once the pork is done, let it rest for 10 minutes before shredding it with two forks.
In a large pot, combine the cooked noodles, shredded pork, and chopped green onions.
Drizzle the infused oil over the top of the ramen and serve immediately.
